calendar.matters:
FUTURE ACCOUNTING
Off Calendar Sep-15-2015 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #25, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Aug-04-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: William Hoehler, for petitioner Union Bank (Trustee)

The Court stated that there were two main issues to address: the purchase of the van without court approval, and investment strategy. Mr. Hoehler replied that in 2003, with Court permission the Trustee had purchased a van for transporting the trust beneficiary, and due to its age the vehicle needed replacement. The vehicle purchased was the same make and had the same features as the one previously purchased, and the entire purchase price came out of income, as the trust income is well in excess of expenses. Regarding investment strategy, Mr. Hoehler stated that the balance of trust is increasing, and now totals over $3,000,000.00. He stated that funds for the trust come from three annuities, and acknowledged that one annuity payment of $16,800.00 was received but not cashed. He suggested that to avoid any recurrence of the incident of failure to cash an annuity payment, the payments be converted if possible to electronic despoit directly into the trust accounts. The Court inquired about scheduling the accounts to be required on an annual basis rather than every two years. Mr. Hoehler replied that his client would comply with what the Court directs. The Court took the matter under submission, directing counsel to submit a revised proposed order.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy K. Kabasares. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #23, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Jan-27-2011 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susanna Poon for Union Bank.

Ms. Poon stated that the amount of $2,950.00 a month was for maintenance, support, and cost for food and clothing. In addition, Ms. Poon stated that the amount of $6,750.00 was for care services that were provided by Ms. Harris and that those fees were increased. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy F. Smith.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #23, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Nov-16-2010 Continued To Jan-11-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Susanna Poon for Union Bank.

The Court informed Ms. Poon that if she was requesting additional fees, that it needed to be done by way of an amended petition. The matter was continued to January 11, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Poon will prepare an amendment to the petition with a hearing date of January 11, 2011.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.
